Every one of those presidents should've done it and actually, as you know, off the record, they're doing it off the record because they haven't got the courage to say it -- those presidents are giving me a lot of credit. "There are those that say that's never happened before, because usually when you're shot down in strong enemy and deep enemy territory, you can't go after them because you'll end up losing 250 people to get one." "So it's a terrible thing. But we had to make a decision, do we do it or do we not? And we did!" tweet

Iran is getting past Israel’s missile defenses by having its ballistic missiles break apart high in the sky, scattering many smaller bombs before interception is possible. Instead of stopping one target, defenses suddenly face dozens, which is far harder and more expensive to deal with—especially for systems like David's Sling. The tactic not only increases the chance of damage on the ground, but also drains limited interceptor supplies. Source: TWZ

Turkish President Erdogan on Netanyahu: I would also like to remind everyone, in the words of our ancestors: “Times change, and power shifts.” Those who undermine peace today merely to extend their political lifespans should not forget that tomorrow they themselves will need peace, justice, and law. History is full of countless examples of this. Most notably, Hitler became a victim of ambitions he could not control, bringing great suffering upon himself, his people, and the world. Those who follow in Hitler’s footsteps today are not only acting against humanity, but are also placing themselves in that same Hitler role against their own citizens.

Turkish President Erdogan on Iran War: We are making a sincere effort to ensure that the weapons fall silent and that space is opened for negotiations. If there is even the smallest chance, we are determined to seize it. Our wish is for this unlawful, meaningless, and illegitimate war—one that is extremely costly for all humanity—to come to an end as soon as possible.
Trump on Iran: War crime is allowing Iran to have a nuclear weapon.

The above video was an FPV attack that impacted the stairs of the door of the Merkava tank, causing minimal damage unfortunately The FPVs in use are all impact based and this forces operators to attack with speed to trigger the munition This issue would be resolved and accuracy will be 99.99% when contact fuzes are developed

↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness: "⚡️🇮🇷🇵🇰🇺🇸IRNA: Iran has submitted a proposed framework to end the war to the U.S. via Pakistan. The proposal reportedly consists of ten clauses and rejects a short-term truce, instead calling for a lasting end to the conflict based on Iran’s terms and security…" ⚡️ 🇮🇷 🇺🇸 🇮🇱 WSJ : Iran has rejected a US‑mediated proposal for a 45‑day ceasefire in exchange for opening the Strait of Hormuz, instead demanding a permanent end to the war. Tehran’s counterproposal includes guarantees for a permanent ceasefire, lifting of sanctions, compensation for war damages, a new arrangement for governing the strait, an end to all regional conflicts, and guarantees that Israel will not target Hezbollah in the future. Mediators warned that if the U.S. strikes Iranian power plants, Iran will retaliate against Israeli and Gulf energy facilities, particularly in Saudi Arabia. @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇮🇷 Israel’s Ministry of Defence announced that the country’s defence industries will significantly increase production and stockpiling of missile interceptors as the war with Iran continues. Production of interceptors for the Arrow system, which defends against long-range ballistic missiles, will be sped up. Officials have maintained there is no shortage of interceptor missiles. @wfwitness
Reporter: Do you expect JD Vance could be involved in an in-person meeting with officials in the coming days? Trump: Could be. Could be. Could be.
